title: Chorus family root spec
description: Machine-readable root for a component family. One file per family at schema/components/<family>/<family>.spec.json. Declares the family's identity, taxonomy axes (form / flavor / variant), and the list of sub-components — so external consumers do not have to crawl the family's prose .md to discover its structure. A family root MUST exist whether the family is single-spec (badge, banner, …) or sub-component (button, tabs, …); single-spec families simply list a single sub-component or none. Sub-component style details live in the matching <sub>.spec.json, not here.

family.description: kebab-case family slug. Matches the folder name under schema/components/ and the `family` key in each sub-spec.

name.description: PascalCase family name (`Button`, `Tabs`, `FormField`). The umbrella React export name.

description.description: One-paragraph family-level role summary — the cross-sub-component intent.

spec.description: Relative path to the family's authoring .md (`button.md`, `tabs.md`, …). Optional but recommended so consumers can link back to the prose.

subcomponents.description: Ordered list of sub-components in the family. The first entry is the canonical default (the form a consumer reaches for when they say "a Button" / "a Chip" / "Tabs"). Single-spec families list exactly one entry whose slug matches the family slug.

slug.description: kebab-case sub-component slug. Resolves to <slug>.md and (when present) <slug>.spec.json in the same folder.

spec.description: Relative path to <slug>.spec.json. Omit only while the sub-component is documented in prose but does not yet have a machine-readable spec — flag this case explicitly with `"specMissing": true` so it shows up in audits.

md.description: Relative path to <slug>.md. Optional for single-spec families where the family .md is also the sub-component .md.

default.description: Marks the canonical default form. Exactly one sub-component per family should set this to true.

specMissing.description: Set to true when the sub-component is intentionally md-only at this point in time. Surfaces in audits as work-to-do.

axes.description: Family-level taxonomy axes that cut across sub-components. The keys are axis names (`form`, `flavor`, `variant`, `appearance`, …); each value lists the canonical enum members. Use this to declare cross-cutting modifiers that aren't visible in a single sub-spec — e.g. button's `flavor: [default, destructive]`.

useCases.description: Free-form intent strings an agent can match against natural-language requests. Used by the catalog (schema/catalog.md) and by intent-driven pickers (Lovable / v0 / Cursor). Examples for `button`: ['primary commit', 'inline action', 'save and continue']. Keep entries short and concrete — synonyms, not full sentences.

visualReuse.description: How strictly the family's intent binds component choice. `open` allows visual-fit reuse outside `useCases`; `locked` restricts the family to its canonical role because the interaction contract is the point. See `AGENTS.md` § Design principles (#5) for the full contract and the locked roster.

layoutInset.description: How the family contributes to the page's horizontal-rail contract — the machine-readable form of the 'one gutter, paid once' rule in AGENTS.md § Composition rules. `full-bleed`: the family stretches edge-to-edge inside the page shell's content box and pays its own internal padding via `layout.container.*` on its rows/header — agents MUST NOT wrap it in an extra `padding-inline` div. `bounded-surface`: the family is its own modal/popover/off-canvas shell (Dialog, BottomSheet, SideSheet, Toast, Tooltip) — it owns its outer padding and is not a sibling of full-bleed page rows. `inline`: slot atom (Button, Badge, Thumbnail, FormField, Header, Chip-as-atom) — has no rail responsibility; the surrounding container places it. Agents read this field to decide whether the family belongs on the shared page rail; recipe validators read it to detect double-padded compositions.

wrapperGuidance.description: One-line agent-facing guidance for composing this family — primarily about the wrapper / padding rule that complements `layoutInset`. Required for `full-bleed` and `bounded-surface` families so agents reading family.json get the no-wrap rule on the same line as the inset classification, without needing to cross-reference `<sub>.md` prose. The text should name the forbidden wrapper shapes (`padding-inline` div, `className="px-*"`, `style={{ padding }}`) and the correct host (page-shell `<main>` for full-bleed, body portal for bounded-surface).

notes.description: Optional free-form note for downstream consumers (e.g. "chip and toolbar share the same visual chrome — contract divergence is intent only").
